Weird and bizarre MIDI devices | 6 best MIDI controllers in 2025
items.editorNotes: The article was rewritten for clarity and readability while maintaining the original factual content.
items.excerptBefore: Traditional MIDI controllers? Yawn.
items.excerptAfter: Explore the fascinating world of unconventional MIDI controllers with our top picks for 2025. Discover unique devices that redefine music creation.
items.seoTitleBefore: Weird and bizarre MIDI devices | 6 best MIDI controllers in 2025
items.seoTitleAfter: Weird and Bizarre MIDI Devices | 6 Best MIDI Controllers in…
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Are you tired of the same old standard gear? If so, you've landed in the perfect spot. Let's explore the weirder side of midi controllers!
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Dive into the world of unconventional MIDI controllers. Check out our top 6 picks for 2025 that will redefine your music-making experience.
items.beforeBody:
Are you tired of the same old standard gear? If so, you've landed in the perfect spot. We're about to celebrate the weirder side of music technology!

They're as commonplace as your average pencil. Today, we’ll rebel against the conventional and explore the mesmerizing world of unconventional MIDI controllers! 

1. Artiphon Orba

The tiny musical marvel that is the Atiphon Orba fits in the palm of your hand and bends to your gestures like an obedient pet. 

The coolest part? 

You can control its many features, such as pitch-bend and filter, with a flick of your wrist or a simple press on one of its 8 pressure-sensitive pads! 

Buy now

2. Playtron

The Playtron MIDI Controller offers a unique way to create your custom MIDI controller. It can connect up to 16 objects with alligator clips. This allows you to use it with several mobile apps, online synthesizers, or your favorite DAW.

From fruits to plants, and even toys, the possibilities are endless. Connect the clips from objects capable of conducting electricity to Playtron, and assign a different MIDI note (C1 to D#2 by default) to each object. Then, you can start playing and creating your unique sounds!

Why we love it

  • A unique way to make music, and you could add a very interesting/interactive aspect to your performance
  • Very affordable price for the several versatile features
  • Combine with other MIDI controllers or instruments to create complex arrangements and performances. 
  • Very portable (can be connected to your computer, tablet, or smartphone via Bluetooth for a wireless setup)

Cons

  • Not best for very precise control
Buy now

3. Lumatone Isomorphic Keyboard

This is the ultimate playground for music creators. With 280 mappable hexagon keys and free Lumatone editing software, you can assign any color, note, tuning, or temperament to each key. 

It's an entirely new way of music-making, making you rethink how you compose melodies and chord progressions. 

Yes, it costs around $4,000, but the Lumatone is built to last with an aluminum body and 10 preset buttons. It's the holy grail for pros who fancy themselves as musical chameleons.

Buy now

4. Dubler Studio Kit 2

Say goodbye to the tedious task of drawing out your drums. With Dubler Studio Kit, you can beatbox your heart out and have it notate your voice into a MIDI format. 

The package comes with a USB microphone and Dubler software, allowing you to turn your voice, and even your hummed bass lines, into MIDI data. 

The mic and software are so responsive that they can work with and distinguish between sounds even when they overlap. 

For the musically challenged, this is a magic wand that turns you into a melody and harmony virtuoso!

Buy now

5. Genki Wave Ring

Ever dreamed of having a musical instrument at your fingertips? Literally? 

Then the Genki Wave Ring is for you. 

This wearable MIDI controller is motion-controlled, has six unique gestures, and is Eurorack compatible. You can control various functions with just a press of a button, making your music production practice more fluid. 

And the best part? 

It's also perfect for live performances, letting you control your music seamlessly with the flick of a finger.

Buy now

6. TouchMe

TouchMe allows you to connect to objects in the same way as Playtron. However, this is a sensory device as it is set to react differently to the touch. Meaning, when the area of touch and intensity changes, the sound changes too, causing the pitch to go higher or lower!

Why we love it

  • Overall great sequencer
  • Perfect amount of keys
  • Combine with other MIDI controllers or instruments to create complex arrangements and performances. 
  • Explore the theremin-like sound generation potential of the TouchMe device to create dynamic and expressive music. 
  • Interact with other people in a musical creative environment.
  • Very portable (can be connected to your computer, tablet, or smartphone via Bluetooth for a wireless setup)

Cons

  • Not best for very precise control
Buy now

Understand the unconventional

From the handheld and gesture-controlled Artiphon Orba to the Lumatone Isomorphic Keyboard that challenges our perception of what a keyboard should look like; these MIDI controllers are as varied as they are unconventional.

Discover the weird and the wonderful

With these MIDI controllers, you can redefine your music game. You can control the Orba with gestures, play the Aerophone on the go, remix the traditional keyboard concept with the Lumatone, translate your voice to MIDI with the Dubler Kit, convert human touch to MIDI with Touchme. You can also use various conductive objects with Playtron or go ultra-tech with the wearable Wave Ring. 

Isn't it time you infused some fun and fresh air into your music life?

items.title: How to master your instrument: expert tips
items.editorNotes: Revised for clarity and readability while maintaining the original content's factual accuracy.
items.excerptBefore: This article covers what it means to master your craft and offers expert tips on how to master your instrument.
items.excerptAfter: Discover expert tips on how to master your instrument, whether you're a beginner or an experienced musician. Learn about setting goals, the importance of music theory, and more.
items.seoTitleBefore: How to master your instrument: expert tips
items.seoTitleAfter: How to Master Your Instrument: Expert Tips
items.seoDescriptionBefore: This article covers what it means to master your craft and offers expert tips on how to master your instrument.
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Explore expert tips on mastering your instrument, including goal setting, music theory, rhythm practice, and genre exploration. Perfect for musicians at…
items.beforeBody:
Whether you’re a beginner or 10 years into learning your instrument, “mastering” your instrument may be one of your end goals as a musician. But how do you get around to becoming a “master” in your instrument?

In this article, we talk about what it means to master your craft and how you can do so. Read on for some expert tips on how to master your instrument.

What does it mean to master your instrument? 

To some, mastering their instrument means being able to improvise and create any melody from the top of their head. To another, it could mean passing a grade assessment of the highest level. So, to begin mastering your instrument, you should ask yourself this: what do I want to achieve with my instrument?

Once you’ve got your broader goal, you can start assessing and aiming for smaller goals that can help you get where you want to be. For example, a complete beginner could make it their ultimate goal to be able to play songs after just listening to them. They would then set smaller goals in between to achieve that. Starting off with learning the correct posture, then moving on to learning how to play some notes, and then some harder notes. They could then progress to working on ear training and learning some music theory. The list goes on!

Looking at your goals as less of an endpoint, and a means to achieve something greater, will be both motivating and satisfying as you progress.

How can you master your instrument?

While the meaning of “mastery” differs for each musician. There are some general tips that can be applied to whatever your goal may be. Here are some of them:

Don’t leave out music theory

Even if you’re not aiming to pass an exam, music theory shouldn’t be ignored. Having just the basics down will be enough to make a difference. Let’s say you’re just starting to learn scales, having some understanding of why the notes are placed where they are and why they mesh well together will take you to the next level. 

Having this comprehension of music theory, even of the most basic and essential levels, will make all the difference between a hobby player and one who’s truly serious about their craft.

Spend some time working on your rhythm

Rhythm is in everything we do. From our heartbeat to the music we listen to every day. It’s no question that mastering rhythm will greatly enhance your abilities. If you’re a beginner, we’ve got a good article on some rhythm exercises for beginners. 

Take some time during each practice session to work on your rhythm. Invest in a good metronome. Sure, mobile apps would work just fine- but if you’re looking for something more, explore the world of metronomes! Here are some exercises you can try to work on your rhythm.

Try different things

Musicians often find themselves confined to a certain genre or style of playing. While you can definitely settle to be just a classical pianist or a jazz guitarist, try exploring some other genres. Getting into styles that you aren’t so familiar with will be both inspiring and challenging. 

Practicing hard doesn’t have to be boring. In fact, the more fun you try to have with each practice session, the better progress you’ll make. Music is a creative process and by trying some other styles you may be able to bring out your own creativity and have some fun during practice.

While it will be challenging to try out a style you’ve never tried before, you will have so much to learn from other musicians and genres. A pianist who exclusively plays classical compositions, for example, will have a lot to learn about improvisation in jazz. 

Remember what you’re playing for

Keep that first goal you had in mind when you’re practicing. Remembering this goal will be like dangling a carrot in front of a donkey’s face. You’ll stay motivated, and your drive will push you harder than you would if you played aimlessly.

This clear vision you have in your mind will help you find your way whenever you feel demotivated or stumped in your progress.

Ultimately, it takes a lot of time and practice to “master” your instrument. But it’s not impossible. All it takes is for you to make the first step in working towards that goal.
items.afterBody:
Whether you’re a beginner or have been learning your instrument for 10 years, mastering it may be one of your ultimate goals as a musician. But how do you become a master of your instrument?

In this article, we discuss what it means to master your craft and provide expert tips on how to achieve that.

What does it mean to master your instrument?

For some, mastering their instrument means being able to improvise and create melodies on the spot. For others, it could mean passing the highest level of a grade assessment. To begin mastering your instrument, ask yourself: what do I want to achieve with my instrument?

Once you have a broader goal, you can set smaller, achievable goals that will help you reach your ultimate aim. For example, a complete beginner might aspire to play songs by ear. They could start with learning proper posture, then progress to playing simple notes, and eventually tackle more complex pieces. Incorporating ear training and music theory into their practice will further enhance their skills.

Viewing your goals as stepping stones rather than endpoints will keep you motivated and satisfied as you progress.

How can you master your instrument?

The meaning of “mastery” varies for each musician, but there are general tips that can apply to any goal. Here are some of them:

Don’t leave out music theory

Even if you’re not preparing for an exam, music theory is essential. Understanding the basics can significantly improve your playing. For instance, if you’re learning scales, knowing why the notes are arranged as they are and how they work together will elevate your skills.

This foundational knowledge of music theory distinguishes a casual player from someone serious about their craft.

Spend some time working on your rhythm

Rhythm is fundamental in everything we do, from our heartbeat to the music we enjoy. Mastering rhythm will enhance your overall abilities. If you’re a beginner, check out our article on rhythm exercises for beginners.

Dedicate time in each practice session to focus on your rhythm. Investing in a good metronome can be beneficial. While mobile apps are convenient, explore the world of metronomes for more options. Here are some exercises to improve your rhythm.

Try different things

Musicians often limit themselves to a specific genre or style. While you may choose to be a classical pianist or a jazz guitarist, exploring other genres can be both inspiring and challenging.

Practicing doesn’t have to be dull. The more enjoyable you make each session, the better your progress will be. Music is a creative process, and experimenting with different styles can enhance your creativity and make practice more fun.

While trying a new style may be challenging, you’ll gain valuable insights from other musicians and genres. For example, a classical pianist can learn a lot about improvisation from jazz.

Remember what you’re playing for

Keep your initial goal in mind while practicing. This goal will serve as motivation, pushing you to work harder than if you were playing aimlessly.

This clear vision will guide you whenever you feel demotivated or stuck in your progress.

Ultimately, mastering your instrument requires time and practice, but it is achievable. All it takes is the first step toward your goal.

items.title: Adrian Valia — Music practice and the Soundbrenner Core 2
items.editorNotes: Ensure all quotes are accurately represented and maintain the original intent.
items.excerptBefore: We went to Los Angeles to meet up with award-winning multi-instrumentalist jazz theorist and author of "Music Theory Made Simple", Adrian Valia to learn more about his thoughts on the Soundbrenner Core 2 and how it helps with music practice.
items.excerptAfter: Adrian Valia shares insights on music practice motivation and the benefits of the Soundbrenner Core 2's features, including the practice tracker and vibrating metronome.

items.seoTitleAfter: Adrian Valia on Music Practice and the Soundbrenner Core 2
items.seoDescriptionBefore: We went to Los Angeles to meet up with award-winning multi-instrumentalist jazz theorist and author of "Music Theory Made Simple", Adrian Valia to learn more about his thoughts on the Soundbrenner Core 2 and how it helps with music practice.
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Discover insights from Adrian Valia on how the Soundbrenner Core 2 enhances music practice motivation through its practice tracker and vibrating metronome…
items.beforeBody:
We went to Los Angeles to meet up with award-winning multi-instrumentalist jazz theorist and author of Music Theory Made Simple, Adrian Valia. We learned more about his thoughts on the Soundbrenner Core 2's newest features such as the practice tracker and how it helps with music practice and motivation.

‘So, how do you stay motivated during music practice?’

“I guess when I struggle with motivation I just try to find the reason why I'm doing music in the first place, and it sounds a bit extreme when sometimes you're just feeling lazy, but you have to keep a very strong goal in mind, like a very strong motivator. If you don't have that, if you're just thinking well I just need to do that to get better. That's not enough for me, I have to go a bit deeper and think — where do I want to see myself in five years, I need to connect with that idea and think that the only way to get there is one step at a time every single day.”

‘How does the Core 2’s practice tracker feature help music practice motivation?’

“It's cool! The fact that you can not only keep yourself accountable, but you can see how many hours you practice and how much closer you are to getting to your particular goals. I think especially with something like practice, which is something that most people do by themselves most of the time, it’s really important to have an external reference and to see how you're progressing — so you make sure that you're getting to where you want to be.”

‘What are the benefits of being able to share your music practice session with a community of musicians?’

“For me, being able to see someone else's journey and see how they're getting to where they want to be, whether that's in terms of consistency or musicianship, is motivating. It only gives me more energy when I'm able to keep myself accountable towards other people and to see how other people are doing as well.”

‘How does the vibrating metronome help with music practice?’

“So the great thing about the Core 2 is the fact that you can feel the rhythm yourself, and you're not relying on external sources. You have to embody that particular tempo which is paramount when you're practicing because of course when you're playing in real life you won't have a click track — if you don't have that it's really important to develop your internal rhythm and I think this is a great tool to get you between having absolutely nothing and having a very annoying metronome, I think this is a great way of internalizing rhythm.”

items.title: Celebrating LGBTQ+ music artists in 2025
items.editorNotes: Ensure to update any links or images if necessary before publishing.
items.excerptBefore: Pride Month may be over, but the celebrations continue! Join us, as we look at some of the most influential LGBTQ music artists of all time.
items.excerptAfter: This article celebrates LGBTQ+ music artists and their influence on culture and community. It highlights notable musicians and their achievements while encouraging year-round appreciation of their contributions.
items.seoTitleAfter: Celebrating LGBTQ+ Music Artists in 2025
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Pride Month may be over, but the celebrations continue! Join us as we explore some of the most influential LGBTQ music artists of all time.
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Explore the impact of LGBTQ+ musicians on music and culture. Discover influential artists and their achievements while celebrating their contributions…
items.beforeBody:
Pride Month may be over, but the celebration of LGBTQ music artists continues. Throughout history, LGBTQ musicians have used their art to express their identities and connect with others. Their music has helped to break down barriers, challenge stereotypes, and inspire people of all sexual orientations and gender identities. In this article, we will take a look at some of the most influential LGBTQ musicians of all time. We will also explore the ways in which their music has helped to shape the LGBTQ community and culture.

Why LGBTQ music artists rock

LGBTQ music artists are not just about the 'glam' and 'glitter'; they're a powerful symphony of talent. Their music is an alchemy of emotion, creativity, and sheer brilliance, with each note they hit resonating with their unique narratives.

These artists pour their heart and soul into every lyric, tune, and rhythm, gifting us masterpieces that move us, uplift us, and sometimes, have us dancing around the kitchen in our PJs at midnight! 

Yes, we’ve all been there, don't even try to deny it.

Music, as they say, is the universal language of humankind. But when it comes from our amazing LGBTQ music artists, it's not just a language; it's an anthem of courage, resilience, and love in all its forms. 

They are the heartbeats that sync with ours and a symphony that echoes our shared humanity.

LGBTQ musicians we adore

Whether you're ready for a dance-off or a soulful serenade, we have some fantastic LGBTQ music artists who've touched our hearts and rocked our world with their magical melodies:

  • Elton John: Our knight in shining piano keys, his magic transcends age and time. Who can resist the charm of "Rocket Man" or "Circle of Life"?
  • Tegan and Sara: These identical twin sisters have stolen our hearts with their indie pop tunes. Their harmonies are perfect, and their lyrics are filled with emotion. They are like a warm cup of cocoa on a rainy day.
  • Frank Ocean: The man who revolutionized R&B with his mesmerizing voice and deeply personal lyrics. Each song is a journey that takes you through the beautiful labyrinth of his experiences and feelings.

But remember, this is just a sample. The spectrum of LGBTQ music artists is as broad and diverse as the rainbow flag that represents them.

LGBTQ music artists by the numbers

Music and numbers? It’s not your typical mix, but let’s jazz it up with some stats! These artists have not just won our hearts but also stormed the music charts with their incredible talent.

Let's keep the beat going

This Pride month, let’s celebrate our favorite LGBTQ music artists by blasting their tunes, sharing their music, and honoring their heroic narratives.

But why stop at Pride Month? 

Let's keep this party going all year round!

Whenever you play a track from one of these LGBTQ music artists, you're not just listening to a song – you're connecting with a story of strength, self-love, and the beautiful spectrum of human experience. 

And, hey, you're also having a great time bopping to the beat. 

Talk about a win-win!

We all need to remember that love is love, music is music, and a catchy tune is still a catchy tune – no matter who sings it. 

Let’s sing it loud, sing it proud, and celebrate our beloved LGBTQ music artists not just today but every day.

Happy Pride, everyone! Keep the music playing, and most importantly, keep the love flowing!

In this article, we will look at some of the most influential LGBTQ+ musicians of all time and explore how their music has shaped the LGBTQ+ community and culture.

Why LGBTQ+ music artists rock

LGBTQ+ music artists are not just about the 'glam' and 'glitter'; they are a powerful symphony of talent. Their music is an alchemy of emotion, creativity, and sheer brilliance, with each note resonating with their unique narratives.

These artists pour their heart and soul into every lyric, tune, and rhythm, gifting us masterpieces that move us, uplift us, and sometimes have us dancing around the kitchen in our PJs at midnight!

Yes, we’ve all been there, don't even try to deny it.

Music, as they say, is the universal language of humankind. But when it comes from our amazing LGBTQ+ music artists, it's not just a language; it's an anthem of courage, resilience, and love in all its forms.

They are the heartbeats that sync with ours and a symphony that echoes our shared humanity.

LGBTQ+ musicians we adore

Whether you're ready for a dance-off or a soulful serenade, we have some fantastic LGBTQ+ music artists who have touched our hearts and rocked our world with their magical melodies:

  • Elton John: Our knight in shining piano keys, his magic transcends age and time. Who can resist the charm of "Rocket Man" or "Circle of Life"?
  • Tegan and Sara: These identical twin sisters have stolen our hearts with their indie pop tunes. Their harmonies are perfect, and their lyrics are filled with emotion. They are like a warm cup of cocoa on a rainy day.
  • Frank Ocean: The man who revolutionized R&B with his mesmerizing voice and deeply personal lyrics. Each song is a journey through the beautiful labyrinth of his experiences and feelings.

But remember, this is just a sample. The spectrum of LGBTQ+ music artists is as broad and diverse as the rainbow flag that represents them.

LGBTQ+ music artists by the numbers

Music and numbers? It’s not your typical mix, but let’s jazz it up with some stats! These artists have not just won our hearts but also stormed the music charts with their incredible talent.

LGBTQ+ Music Artists Notable Achievements Elton John Over 300 million records sold worldwide Tegan and Sara 3 Juno Awards and a Grammy nomination Frank Ocean His debut album, "Channel Orange," won a Grammy

Let's keep the beat going

This Pride Month, let’s celebrate our favorite LGBTQ+ music artists by blasting their tunes, sharing their music, and honoring their heroic narratives.

But why stop at Pride Month?

Let's keep this party going all year round!

Whenever you play a track from one of these LGBTQ+ music artists, you're not just listening to a song – you're connecting with a story of strength, self-love, and the beautiful spectrum of human experience.

And, hey, you're also having a great time bopping to the beat.

Talk about a win-win!

We all need to remember that love is love, music is music, and a catchy tune is still a catchy tune – no matter who sings it.

Let’s sing it loud, sing it proud, and celebrate our beloved LGBTQ+ music artists not just today but every day.

Happy Pride, everyone! Keep the music playing, and most importantly, keep the love flowing!

items.seoTitleAfter: Top 5 Best Musician Apps in 2025
items.seoDescriptionBefore: To help you choose the best musician apps, we have listed our top 5 picks for the best music apps to take inspiration from.

items.beforeBody:
Whether you're a professional musician or a passionate beginner. The right apps on your smartphone or tablet can significantly influence your musical journey. In the pool of options available, you might get stuck on which app to side with. To help you choose the best musician apps, we have listed some music apps to take inspiration from.

Top 5 best musician apps in 2025

1. Metronome by Soundbrenner

Metronome by Soundbrenner is a fantastic app that brings the classic metronome into the digital realm.

It is a popular wearable device designed for musicians to assist with rhythm and control. Metronome features a sleek graphical interface along with custom options to meet every musician’s expectations.

Features

  • Customizable Metronome
  • Visuals feedback
  • Rhythm Coach
  • Set list and Song Mode
  • Tap Tempo and Beat Detection

2. Volume Booster

Whether you're practicing in a noisy environment or performing on stage. Having a sound booster app can be a game-changer for musicians.

Volume booster facilitates you with amplification of voice and instruments, without compromising on the quality.

These apps ensure that your music cuts through the noise and reaches the audience with clarity and impact.

Features

  • Transparent Sound
  • Equalization Option
  • Active/Passive Compatibility
  • Durability

3. Bandlab

Bandlab is the app you must have to enhance your music horizon. Since, collaboration is the key for musicians.

Bandlab is a comprehensive online music production platform. That offers you to connect with fellow artists, songwriters, and producers. It serves as a social platform where musicians can create, record, and share their music with a global community.

Features

  • Digital Audio Workstation
  • Collaboration Tools
  • Cloud Storage and Backup
  • Mixing and Mastering
  • Social Network and Community
  • Educational Resource

4. iReal Pro

iReal Pro is a popular music app designed for musicians and music enthusiasts to improvise their existing skills.

It possesses a vast library of chord progressions and backing tracks across various genres. iReal Pro is like a personal coach you can have in your pocket.

Features

  • Realistic Accompanist
  • Customizable Chord Charts
  • Practice Tools
  • Transportation and playback options
  • Songbook and Playlist Management
  • Integration With External devices

5. Guitar Tuna

This music app assists you with tuning and improving your guitar playing. Guitar Tuna is a highly acclaimed app that offers advanced tuning capabilities for a wide range of instruments, including guitar, bass, ukulele, and more.

The app features precise tuning algorithms, making it a reliable option for musicians and guitarists to learn and practice.

Features

  • Game-like Interface
  • Chord Diagram
  • Scales and Exercises
  • Metronome
  • Songbook

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1. What is the best app to play along with music?

Answer: There are many excellent music apps available to play along with music. Garage Band, Bandlab, Soundtrap, and Yousician are a few apps you can use along with the music.

Q2. Do musicians memorize their music?

Answer: Yes, musicians memorize their music as it leverages their performance. It helps them to connect more deeply with music, focus on expressive interpretation, and engage the audience.

Q3. What do musicians read?

Answer: Musicians read various types of musical notes that help them perform better on stage. Sheet music, musical scores, chord charts, lead sheets, tablature, and music theory books are a few to name.

items.title: 5 effective techniques to enhance your music production in 2025
items.editorNotes: The article has been rewritten for clarity and readability while maintaining the original meaning and factual content.
items.excerptBefore: Want to elevate your tracks? Look no further! - Here's our list of 5 music production tips to help you on your journey to success
items.excerptAfter: Explore five effective techniques to elevate your music production skills in 2025, from collaborating with other producers to experimenting with different genres.
items.seoTitleBefore: 5 effective techniques to enhance your music production in 2025
items.seoTitleAfter: 5 Effective Techniques to Enhance Your Music Production in…
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Want to elevate your tracks? Look no further! - Here's our list of 5 music production tips to help you on your journey to success
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Discover five essential tips to improve your music production skills in 2025. Learn about collaboration, sound refinement, and genre experimentation.
items.beforeBody:
Calling all music producers! Want to elevate your tracks and perfect your craft? Look no further! We get it — you want to create music that captivates and stands out. That’s why we’ve put together five must-know music production tips to help you on your journey to success. So, don’t delay, start implementing them today!

How can I enhance my skills in sound production?

1. Team up with other producers

Maximize your collaborations by teaming up with someone who shares your style but practices a different subgenre or genre. By doing so, you can tap into fresh audiences while pushing your creative boundaries. Say, for instance, you're a dubstep pro – pairing up with a hip-hop producer can showcase your expertise in crafting heavy basslines and injecting new, imaginative ideas. Celebrate your individuality and merge it with a new context to create something truly one-of-a-kind — a result that neither of you could have achieved alone!

Improve your music collaboration by paying attention to your partner's approach to songwriting, sound design, and production. Take note of what impresses you, what is unexpected, and what may not seem promising, and use that knowledge to enhance your tracks. Your partner's experience and insights are invaluable, so share and learn from one another. Remember, your unique style can be the missing piece your partner needs. Keep growing together for exceptional results.

2. Try not to correct the music through the equalizer

Improve your music by refining your sound before adjusting the equalizer. Many producers skip this crucial step and end up distorting the overall quality by over-equalizing their sound.

To succeed in music production, prioritize frequencies during pre-mixing. Gifted producers adjust the frequency response of the sound, rather than removing low frequencies. By using a higher octave or different sound, you can auto-adjust the frequency response and fit better into the mix.

Discovering the right sounds for your music can be a daunting task, but with the right mindset, it can become much easier. By selecting sounds that naturally fit within your frequency range, rather than forcing them into a mix, you'll craft better music. If you're missing mid-range tones, choose or create a mid-range dominant synth sound with notes that highlight this range. Trust us, this approach will simplify the arrangement, mixing, and mastering process, ultimately resulting in music that sounds amazing.

(Quick tip on using the internet to its max)

The fastest way to find data on the internet is to use ChatGPT. However, you may not have access to it due to regional restrictions. To get access to ChatGPT, you can use a VPN. If you want ChatGPT unblocked, connect to a remote server in a country where it's allowed. VeePN with its 2500+ servers will help with this. ChatGPT can be useful in many ways when you're making your music. For example, you can ask what are the 7 key elements of music production and instantly get an answer in an accessible language.

3. Try yourself in a completely different genre

Revamp your music production process by experimenting with a new style or genre. Take a break from your routine and reset yourself creatively. Whether you choose to release the song under your artist's name is entirely up to you. Don't worry about perfection — your first attempt may not sound great, and that's okay. The key is to finish it quickly in just a few sittings to avoid getting sidetracked. Try a new style that's far from your usual sound, like switching from EDM to metal or from hip-hop to orchestral. Afterward, take notes on what worked and what didn't.

4. There is nothing wrong with using samples

Discovering inspiration, igniting creativity, and producing superior music are achievable with samples. However, the key component that should never be disregarded is that exceptional samples lead to a better rhythm.

While lower-quality, gritty samples may seem enticing, solely utilizing them will result in subpar tracks. Instead, strive for high-quality samples and rely on post-processing techniques to adjust the sound to the desired effect. Bit reduction, for instance, offers a cleaner result that imitates the sound of an old song remastered.

But, the issue at hand is not only sound quality – it's about producing music that captivates the audience and leaves a lasting impression. Invest in top-notch samples to achieve ultimate success in your music production.

5. Pushing every effect to extremes

Mastering a new audio plugin can be intimidating, but there are ways to understand its capabilities fully. One approach is to gradually increase the different parameters to their maximum levels; just make sure to use a safety limiter to protect your ears. Sometimes it can be difficult to deduce the function of each knob or fader, but don't worry. The Ultimate Guide to Audio Effects is a tremendous resource that can help. Moreover, don't hesitate to experiment with unconventional techniques, like using Finisher RETRO's Organizer preset to add distortion to strings or adding a long delay to a bassline recorded with Virtual Bassist DANDY. The majority of the time, trying something unexpected has no negative implications.

Conclusion

Creating great music is a time-consuming and dedicated process. It necessitates grasping the basics of sound production, the utilization of potent software plugins, and experimenting with diverse sounds to forge something exceptional. Most importantly, respect your musical inspirations when breaking new ground with your music! Hopefully, these tips have helped you in some way; happy producing!

items.title: All new Playtron and TouchMe MIDI controllers!
items.editorNotes: Ensure all links and images are functional and relevant.
items.excerptBefore: Introducing Playtron and TouchMe MIDI controllers by Soundbrenner. These will enitrely change your perception of music and MIDI controllers!
items.excerptAfter: Discover the innovative Playtron and TouchMe MIDI controllers that unleash your musical creativity. Perfect for musicians of all levels, these tools offer unique ways to interact with sound.
items.seoTitleBefore: All new Playtron and TouchMe MIDI controllers!
items.seoTitleAfter: Explore Playtron and TouchMe MIDI Controllers
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Introducing Playtron and TouchMe MIDI controllers by Soundbrenner. These will change your perception of music and MIDI controllers!
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Unleash your musical creativity with Playtron and TouchMe MIDI controllers. Perfect for all musicians, these innovative tools offer unique sound…
items.beforeBody:
Looking for new and exciting ways to unleash your musical creativity? Look no further than Playtron and TouchMe. Playtronica is the perfect MIDI controller for any musician, from beginners to experienced professionals. You can revolutionize your music with Playtron and TouchMe MIDI controllers that will help unleash your musical creativity in exciting new ways.

These innovative controllers offer a unique and intuitive way to interact with music. Thus, allowing you to experiment with sounds and textures in ways never before possible with traditional keyboard and mouse-based music production.

Playtron MIDI Controller

The Playtron MIDI Controller offers a unique way to create your own custom MIDI controller. It has the ability to connect up to 16 objects with alligator clips. This allows you to use it with several mobile apps, online synthesizers, or your favorite DAW.

From fruits to plants, and even toys, the possibilities are endless. Connect the clips from objects capable of conducting electricity to Playtron, and assign a different MIDI note (C1 to D#2 by default) to each object. Then, you can start playing and creating your own unique sounds!

Buy now

TouchMe

TouchMe allows you to connect to objects in the same way as Playtron. However, this is a sensory device as it is set to react differently to the touch. Meaning, that when the area of touch and intensity changes, the sound changes too, causing the pitch to go higher or lower!

Buy now

Exploring New Creative Dimensions

These controllers offer endless possibilities for creativity. 

  • Use them to trigger generative patches and embrace a probabilistic approach to making music that you can react to. 
  • Create and play your instruments with everyday objects.
  • Connect to objects to tell a story and create interactive audiovisual projects in the studio or the classroom. 
  • Trigger notes, sound textures or video in the context of an art installation.
  • Combine with other MIDI controllers or instruments to create complex arrangements and performances. 
  • Explore the theremin-like sound generation potential of the TouchMe device to create dynamic and expressive music. 
  • Interact with other people in a musical creative environment.

Main Features

Playtron and TouchMe MIDI controllers are not only easy to use with intuitive controls, but they are also highly portable. In fact, they can be connected to your computer, tablet, or smartphone via Bluetooth for a wireless setup. Additionally, you can experiment with a wide range of objects to create your custom MIDI controller and assign and play specific notes associated with each object with Playtron. Moreover, you can configure TouchMe’s sensitivity for increased pitch accuracy and use it with an online synth or with any VST instrument inside your DAW.

These powerful, versatile, and intuitive tools allow you to create music in a natural and intuitive way. Consequently, whether you are a seasoned musician, educator, or just starting, Playtron and TouchMe are the perfect tools to explore new creative dimensions and discover the music you can create.

 

Pedro Murino Almeida is an Award-Winning composer and experienced music teacher. He maintains a blog called Beyond Music Theory to provide tools and instruction for beginning to advanced music students. Equally, for those wishing to learn about music theory, how to use it and improve their songwriting and music production skills.

items.title: A brief history of music: Happy world music day!
items.editorNotes: Revised for clarity and readability while maintaining factual accuracy and original links.
items.excerptAfter: Celebrate World Music Day with a nostalgic journey through the history of music, from prehistoric tunes to modern genres.
items.seoTitleBefore: A brief history of music: Happy world music day!
items.seoTitleAfter: A Brief History of Music: Happy World Music Day!
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Enter the Music Time Machine! On this glorious World Music Day, let's get our helmets on and take a ride through the history of music.
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Join us as we explore the fascinating history of music, celebrating World Music Day with insights from prehistoric times to modern genres.
items.beforeBody:
Enter the Music Time Machine! On this glorious World Music Day, let's get our time-travel helmets on and take a nostalgic ride through the resounding history of music. 

We’ll take a quick look at:

  • Prehistoric: Cavemen's rockin' rhythm
  • Classical: Mozart, Bach, and Beethoven's power trio
  • Jazz: The rebellious teen, breaking all rules
  • Rock 'n' Roll: Turning it up to 11
  • Modern Era: A smorgasbord of sounds to savour

Join us on this ride through the history of music at break-neck speed.

Prehistoric pre-tunes

First stop, way back in time! I'm talking before 'Backstreet Boys' or 'The Beatles,' even before Beethoven. This was when humans first realized that banging rocks could sound pretty rad. Prehistoric Music is our earliest known form of tunes, with the oldest instrument, a flute made from a vulture's wing bone, dating back to 35,000 years ago. Cavemen with flutes, who would've thought?

The classic classics

Next, let's fast-forward to the classic era of Classical Music. Picture a fancy wig, a huge crowd, and a booming orchestra – yes, we're talking about the likes of Mozart, Bach, and Beethoven! These dudes knew how to throw a party with a symphony, inventing and refining music genres we still rock today.

The jazz jamboree

And just when you thought the music scene was getting a little predictable, in came the foot-tapping, hip-swaying Jazz! This sensational music style boogied its way from the African American communities of New Orleans in the late 19th and early 20th centuries. It was like a rebellious teen of the music world, breaking all the classical rules and making people move as they'd just discovered dancing. Thank you, Jazz!

The rock 'n' roll revolution

From the humble blues and gospel beginnings, there erupted a genre that transformed music forever – Rock 'n' Roll. In the mid-1950s, artists like Elvis Presley and Chuck Berry were all like, "Hey, let's take this music thing and turn it up to 11!". And thus, a whole new wave of music began, and man, we've been headbanging since!

Pop culture powerhouse

And here we are now, in the era where anything goes! Music genres have exploded, giving us an awe-inspiring variety of tunes to jam to. We’ve got pop, hip-hop, reggae, country, techno, punk, EDM, and the list goes on. Each genre has its own unique charm and style, providing the soundtrack to our lives.

Let’s crank up the volume

Music is the universal language that we all speak. It’s the beat of our hearts, the rhythm of our lives, and the melody of our souls. So, on this World Music Day, let's crank up the volume and celebrate the power of music! And, remember, you don't need a time machine to take a trip through the history of music. Just a killer playlist, a good pair of headphones, and an adventurous spirit! 

Now, go forth and rock on! 

Happy World Music Day, folks!

Thanks for reading our article, "A Brief History of Music: Happy World Music Day!" If you'd like to explore more of our articles, check out "The World's Most Beautiful Music Venues: A Visual Journey."

  • Jazz: The rebellious teen, breaking all rules
  • Rock 'n' Roll: Turning it up to 11
  • Modern Era: A smorgasbord of sounds to savour

Join us on this ride through the history of music at break-neck speed.

Prehistoric pre-tunes

First stop, way back in time! I'm talking before 'Backstreet Boys' or 'The Beatles,' even before Beethoven. This was when humans first realized that banging rocks could sound pretty rad. Prehistoric Music is our earliest known form of tunes, with the oldest instrument, a flute made from a vulture's wing bone, dating back to 35,000 years ago. Cavemen with flutes, who would've thought?

The classic classics

Next, let's fast-forward to the classic era of Classical Music. Picture a fancy wig, a huge crowd, and a booming orchestra – yes, we're talking about the likes of Mozart, Bach, and Beethoven! These composers knew how to throw a party with a symphony, inventing and refining music genres we still enjoy today.

The jazz jamboree

And just when you thought the music scene was getting a little predictable, in came the foot-tapping, hip-swaying Jazz! This sensational music style boogied its way from the African American communities of New Orleans in the late 19th and early 20th centuries. It was like a rebellious teen of the music world, breaking all the classical rules and making people move as they'd just discovered dancing. Thank you, Jazz!

The rock 'n' roll revolution

From the humble blues and gospel beginnings, there erupted a genre that transformed music forever – Rock 'n' Roll. In the mid-1950s, artists like Elvis Presley and Chuck Berry were all about turning the music up to 11. Thus, a whole new wave of music began, and man, we've been headbanging since!

Pop culture powerhouse

And here we are now, in the era where anything goes! Music genres have exploded, giving us an awe-inspiring variety of tunes to jam to. We’ve got pop, hip-hop, reggae, country, techno, punk, EDM, and the list goes on. Each genre has its own unique charm and style, providing the soundtrack to our lives.

Let’s crank up the volume

Music is the universal language that we all speak. It’s the beat of our hearts, the rhythm of our lives, and the melody of our souls. So, on this World Music Day, let's crank up the volume and celebrate the power of music! Remember, you don't need a time machine to take a trip through the history of music. Just a killer playlist, a good pair of headphones, and an adventurous spirit!

Now, go forth and rock on!

Happy World Music Day, folks!

Thanks for reading our article, "A Brief History of Music: Happy World Music Day!" If you'd like to explore more of our articles, check out "The World's Most Beautiful Music Venues: A Visual Journey."

items.title: 7 rockin’ Father’s Day gifts for music-loving dads
items.editorNotes: Rewritten for clarity and readability while preserving original links and factual content.
items.excerptAfter: Discover the ultimate guide to the best Father's Day gifts for music-loving dads, featuring unique and practical items that will keep him playing and smiling all year long.
items.seoTitleAfter: 7 Rockin' Father's Day Gifts for Music-Loving Dads
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Father's Day is right around the corner! Here are 7 ideas for Father's Day gifts to hit all the right notes and get those dad feet tapping.
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Explore the best Father's Day gifts for musicians, from premium in-ear monitors to online music lessons, ensuring your dad enjoys his passion for music.
items.beforeBody:
Father’s Day is coming up fast (depending on where you are), and if you’re hunting for the perfect present for your music-loving dad, look no further. Here’s your ultimate guide to the best Father’s Day gifts for musicians, designed to keep him playing, grooving, and smiling all year long.

1. Soundbrenner Wave IEMs: Premium in-ear monitors for crystal-clear sound

The Soundbrenner Wave IEMs are the ultimate Father’s Day gift for dads who take their sound seriously. These premium in-ear monitors deliver mind-blowing audio and incredible noise isolation, making them the best headphones for musicians who want to hear every detail.

Why we love them:

  • Studio-quality audio that makes every note pop

  • Superior noise isolation for complete focus

  • Durable build and sleek, modern style

  • Comes with a rugged case for gigs and travel

If your dad’s into recording, rehearsing, or just relaxing with tunes, this will blow him away.

2. Soundbrenner Core 2 or Pulse wearable metronome: Never miss a beat

For a Father’s Day gift that’s both unique and practical, check out the Soundbrenner Core 2 or the Soundbrenner Pulse. These wearable metronomes keep Dad on time, whether he’s practicing solo or jamming with the band.

  • Core 2: A 5-in-1 tool combining a metronome, smartwatch, dB meter, tuner, and practice tracker – everything a musician needs in one sleek package.

  • Pulse: A streamlined vibrating metronome that helps him keep perfect time without a distracting click.

Perfect for dads who want to tighten up their rhythm and keep the groove locked in.

3. Minuendo earplugs: Protect his most important instrument – his ears

For a thoughtful Father’s Day gift, consider the Minuendo Lossless Earplugs. These earplugs are the best for musicians who care about their hearing – they protect without sacrificing sound quality.

Why we love them:

  • Stepless volume adjustment – dial in the perfect level

  • Crystal-clear sound with zero loss

  • Comfortable for long sessions

  • Essential ear protection for concerts, practice, or loud jams

4. Fender Mustang Micro: The perfect portable amp for on-the-go jams

The Fender Mustang Micro is a game-changer for any guitarist or bassist dad. This compact, headphone guitar amp lets him plug in and rock out anywhere.

Why it’s one of the best Father’s Day gifts for guitarists:

  • 12 amp models and effects for endless tone options

  • Bluetooth audio streaming to play along with his favorite songs

  • Ultra-portable and easy to use – no setup, no stress

  • Legendary Fender tone in a pocket-sized package

5. Guitar Aerobics by Hal Leonard: Boost his chops with daily challenges

If Dad’s always looking to level up his playing, Guitar Aerobics  is the ultimate practice tool. It’s a full year of daily guitar exercises to keep him shredding like a pro.

Why this book is a perfect Father’s Day gift for guitar players:

  • Builds strength, dexterity, and technique

  • Covers scales, arpeggios, picking patterns, and more

  • Keeps practice interesting and fresh

  • Suitable for electric and acoustic guitars

6. Concert tickets: Let him see his favorite band live

Experiences make unforgettable gifts. Treat Dad to an epic night out with a Vivid Seats gift card – the easiest way to see his favorite artists live.

Why we love this gift:

  • Flexible choice – he picks the show

  • 100% buyer guarantee for peace of mind

  • The excitement of live music, up close and personal

7. Private online music lessons: Take his skills to the next level

For dads who want to pick up a new instrument or master a new technique, Virtu Academy’s online music lessons are the perfect Father’s Day present. Certified instructors tailor lessons to his goals – all from home.

Why it’s a great gift idea:

  • Convenient online format – no commute, no stress

  • Works for beginners and seasoned players alike

  • Choose from single lessons or full courses

Make this Father’s Day epic

There you have it – 7 of the best Father’s Day gifts for musicians, plus a bonus idea to really make his day. Whether he’s a guitarist, a gear junkie, or just loves live music, these gifts will keep his musical spirit alive.

items.title: 5 best headphone amps for silent practice: A comprehensive guide
items.editorNotes: The article has been rewritten for clarity and readability while maintaining the original content and links.
items.excerptBefore: Looking to get yourself the best headphone amplifier out there? We have compiled a list of the 5 best headphone amps for silent practice!
items.excerptAfter: Discover the top 5 headphone amps for silent practice, perfect for musicians of all levels. Enhance your skills without noise complaints and enjoy crisp sound with these handpicked options.
items.seoTitleBefore: 5 best headphone amps for silent practice: A comprehensive guide
items.seoTitleAfter: 5 Best Headphone Amps for Silent Practice
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Looking to get yourself the best headphone amplifier out there? We have compiled a list of the 5 best headphone amps for silent practice!
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Explore our comprehensive guide on the best headphone amps for silent practice. Perfect for musicians of all levels, these amps enhance your skills without…
items.beforeBody:
by guitarguitar.co.uk

 

Strumming away in a cramped space? Fear not, fellow six-string slingers! Headphone amps for hush-hush practice are here to save the day. A nifty headphone amp will let you sharpen your skills without any noise complaints, and they even make your tunes sound crispier!

Feast your eyes on our top 5 headphone amps, handpicked for musicians of all levels – from newbie strummers to guitar gods in disguise. We've weighed up factors like sound quality, price, and user-friendliness to curate this rockin' list of the finest headphone amps out there. So, dive in and find your perfect match as it's time to revolutionize your practice sessions and unleash your inner maestro, all in sweet, sweet silence.

Top 5 Headphone Amps

Silent practice is a guitarist's best friend, and a top-notch headphone amp makes all the difference. Behold our top 5 picks for the finest headphone amps to keep your jam sessions hush-hush:

1. Fender Mustang Micro

Tiny but mighty, the Fender Mustang Micro packs a punch with its array of amp models and effects. Charge it up with USB-C and even use it as a recording interface! Get ready to rock with classic Fender tones, reverb, delay, and modulation. Plus, it's got a headphone jack and aux input for jamming along with your favorite tracks.

 

Buy now

2. NUX Mighty Plug Pro Headphone Amplifier

Unleash the power of the NUX Mighty Plug Pro, a versatile headphone amp boasting tons of amp models, effects, and nifty practice tools. Rock out wirelessly with the rechargeable battery and NUX Mighty app, tweaking the amp models and effects on the go. Feeling rhythmic? The app's metronome and drum machine has got you covered. And, of course, it comes with a headphone jack and aux input for those backing track jam sessions.

 

Buy now

3. Valeton Rushead Max Pocket Guitar Practice Amp

Introducing the Valeton Rushead Max, a pint-sized powerhouse for your headphone jam sessions! With a variety of amp models and effects inspired by classic tube amps, you'll be rockin' reverb, delay, and modulation like a pro. Plus, it's got a rechargeable battery, a headphone jack for hush-hush practice, a built-in tuner, and a metronome. Who knew so much awesomeness could fit in your pocket?

 

Buy now

4. NUX Mighty Plug Headphone Amplifier with Bluetooth

Meet the NUX Mighty Plug, your new best friend for portable guitar greatness. This little gadget boasts a range of amp models and effects based on iconic tube amps, plus reverb, delay, and modulation. What's more? It's got a rechargeable battery, Bluetooth capabilities for wireless fun, a headphone jack, and an aux-in for jamming with backing tracks. Mighty indeed!

 

Buy now

5. Darkglass Element Headphone Amp and Cabinet Simulator

Say hello to the Darkglass Element, a top-notch headphone amp and cabinet simulator that serves up a smorgasbord of amp models and effects. With spatial technology and a gyro sensor, you'll enjoy an immersive playing experience that's nothing short of magical. Go wireless with the Darkglass Tone app, where you can control your sound and access nifty practice tools like a metronome and drum machine. And, of course, it's got a headphone jack and aux for rocking out with your favorite tunes.

 

Buy now

An Accessory to Adore — Soundbrenner Core 2

Behold, the Soundbrenner Core 2 – the ultimate sidekick for headphone amp-wielding musicians! This snazzy wearable gizmo uses powerful vibrations to keep you in sync with your tunes, even with headphones on. With an inbuilt vibrating metronome, contact tuner, dB meter, and smartphone functions, the Soundbrenner Core is any musician's ultimate practice companion.

Moreover, the Core’s sync feature lets you connect multiple devices, so your entire band or ensemble can rock in unison. Thanks to the Soundbrenner Core 2, you can practice with confidence, knowing you're always right on time.

Conclusion

To wrap it up, our top 5 headphone amps for hushed practice cater to musicians from rookies to rock stars, boasting unique features for every need. Choosing the perfect amp can be daunting, but our research and recommendations aim to make your decision a breeze. When picking a headphone amp, think about what matters most to you—portability, tone, built-in effects, or simplicity. 

Top contenders include Fender Mustang Micro for budget-friendliness and sound precision, Valeton Rushead Max Pocket for superb tone, and NUX Mighty Plug with Bluetooth for wireless freedom and immersive experiences. Darkglass Element Headphone Amp and Cabinet Simulator also offer versatile, high-quality options. Whichever amp you choose, remember to prioritize practice and stay committed to your musical dreams. Rock on!

items.title: Thriving remotely: The power of digital collaboration
items.editorNotes: Ensure all links are functional and images are displayed correctly.
items.excerptBefore: In this article, we discuss the power of digital collaboration and how it can be super easy in the current world of AI!
items.excerptAfter: Explore the transformative impact of digital collaboration in the music industry, highlighting tools and platforms that enable seamless remote work.
items.seoTitleBefore: Thriving remotely: The power of digital collaboration
items.seoTitleAfter: Thriving Remotely: The Power of Digital Collaboration in…
items.seoDescriptionBefore: In this article, we discuss the power of digital collaboration and how it can be super easy in the current world of AI!
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Discover how digital collaboration is reshaping the music industry, enabling remote work and innovative creative processes through advanced tools and…
items.beforeBody:
by Tero Potila

The Power of Digital Collaboration: I've been involved in music in one way or another all of my life. For the past 15+ years, I've worked full-time in the music industry as a composer, producer, and songwriter. 

Over the years, how we work and create music has undergone a seismic shift, with remote work becoming increasingly prevalent. One of the critical aspects of the creative process is collaboration. Traditionally, this has required everyone to be in the same room.

In-Person Collaboration Remotely

Today, we have digital tools that make remote collaboration almost as seamless as being there in person. I regularly do writing sessions with participants from all across the globe. 

I recently did a session from my home studio in Florida, where one co-writer was in Los Angeles, CA, and another in London, UK. The vocals were recorded in Los Angeles while I produced the final music in my studio.

The Power of Digital Tools

Today, we have access to many digital tools that have reshaped how we create music, opening the door to new possibilities.

There are now affordable options for all our creative tools: A home recording studio setup, the instruments for performing the music, and the software tools needed to deliver a broadcast-quality final product. 

We also have emulation software that brings back the classic warmth of analogue into the digital world. In the past, only significant labels could access tools like these.

However, technology advancements have made it possible to record professional quality ideas, even directly on your phone. In addition, you can have a pulsing metronome on your wrist that doesn't distract the session with an audible sound.

Buy now

 

As a result, online platforms deliver the music directly to the consumer. Furthermore, playlists with algorithms and curators help connect fans with themes that match their tastes in the ever-expanding catalog available for streaming.

Key Digital Tools for Remote Collaboration in Music

There's a wide array of digital tools at our disposal. The more familiar tools include online repositories like Dropbox and Google Drive for file sharing and communication apps like Slack or Zoom. Combine these apps with a noise-cancelling microphone, and you have the perfect setup for collaborating remotely.

Music Collaboration Platforms

Beyond these traditional sharing tools, there are platforms tailored explicitly for live recording sessions. These platforms allow you to stream and record high-quality audio over the internet, and they combine screen-sharing capabilities and live video and audio chats. 

One such platform worth mentioning is Listento; a plugin used directly within your Digital Audio Workstation (DAW). It lets you send and receive audio over the internet 'live' within your DAW project. Listento makes it possible to stream, record, and collaborate in real time inside your DAW.

Conclusion

As we look to the future, it's evident that remote work will continue to shape the music industry. No doubt, AI technology will also bring us further advancements in digital collaboration. The digital tools at our disposal today enable live collaborations on a global level.



Tero is a professional music composer and producer. His career combining knowledge and experience from music, TV, film, ad, and game industries gives him a unique perspective that he shares through posts on teropotila.com.

items.title: Soundbrenner Core 2 debut: The wearable for all musicians!
items.editorNotes: The article has been rewritten for clarity and readability while maintaining the original facts and links.
items.excerptBefore: Soundbrenner debuts Core 2, the wearable device for all musicians that makes music practice (almost) addictive
items.excerptAfter: Soundbrenner launches the Core 2, a wearable device designed to enhance practice for musicians with new features and a tracking app.
items.seoTitleBefore: Soundbrenner Core 2 debut: The wearable for all musicians!
items.seoTitleAfter: Soundbrenner Core 2: The Ultimate Wearable for Musicians
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Soundbrenner debuts Core 2, the wearable device for all musicians that makes music practice (almost) addictive
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Discover the Soundbrenner Core 2, a revolutionary wearable designed to help musicians practice effectively with new features and a tracking app. Pre-order…
items.beforeBody:
Launching a crowdfunding campaign today on Kickstarter, Core 2 by Soundbrenner helps musicians to remove the hassle from practice, build a practice habit and reach their musical goals.

Berlin, Germany – May 24th, 2023

Until now, we focused our wearables on reinventing traditional music tools like the metronome, tuner, and decibel meter in addition to being stylish watches for musicians. Today, this focus expands to the realm of habit-building and quantified self with a brand-new tracking app. The device also ships with the new Core 2 exclusive operating system. It comes with highly-requested features such as a new microphone tuner app, music playback controls for smartphones, and many quality-of-life improvements. In addition to these new features, the operating system also includes a number of bug fixes and performance improvements.

Alongside the Core 2 launches a new magnetic contact tuning clip accessory. In addition, the Core 2 comes in two brand-new finishes and colors: ivory-white polycarbonate with a silicone strap and carbon gray steel with a premium leather strap. Furthermore, the Core 2 is now available for pre-order with worldwide shipping and special pricing starting from $159, saving $70 from future retail pricing. Finally, it is designed for all musicians, from beginners to professionals.

Major additions

The Core 2's new tracking app helps musicians practice more effectively. Track your practice time, set goals, and compare your progress to motivating graphs. The app encourages you to hit uninterrupted practice streaks and provides practice reminders. There's no excuse not to practice like a pro with the Core 2's tracking app!

The device also ships with the new Core 2 exclusive operating system, which comes with a range of highly requested features. Users can conveniently access the new music playback controls feature from the watch's face. When listening to music on their smartphones, users can adjust the volume, choose songs and toggle the playback right from the convenience of their wrist while leaving their phones in their pockets.

In addition…

Core 2 now features a new microphone tuner app. This opens up tuning compatibility with more instruments than were previously available with Core 1's contact tuning feature. So, now all musicians can always sound great.

People who need to tune in loud environments can unlock a contact tuning feature. All it requires is a magnetic tuning accessory that is sold separately. Musicians can choose from worry-free tuner mounts with an adhesive that always remains attached to the instrument. Or the brand-new magnetic tuner clip that allows them to easily tune multiple instruments for musicians with a big arsenal.

Core 2 includes a wide range of quality-of-life improvements and features that musicians love from previous generations. These features include a vibrating metronome, a dB meter with a dB alarm, support for smartphone notifications, a timer, a stopwatch, and an alarm clock.

With its sleek design and versatile features, the Soundbrenner Core 2 is set to revolutionize the way musicians practice and achieve their musical goals. It's now available for pre-order with worldwide shipping and special pricing starting from $159, saving $70 from future retail pricing.

Pre-order now

Social media for musicians: Grow your audience with videos
items.editorNotes: Revised for clarity and readability while maintaining original content and links.
items.excerptBefore: Social media for musicians is a new but fast-growing trend. In this article, we'll show you how to jump on it. Read to learn more!
items.excerptAfter: Explore creative ideas for musicians to enhance their social media presence through video content.
items.seoTitleBefore: Social media for musicians: Grow your audience with videos
items.seoTitleAfter: Social Media for Musicians: Grow Your Audience with Videos
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Social media for musicians is a new but fast-growing trend. In this article, we'll show you how to jump on it. Read to learn more!
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Discover effective strategies for musicians to enhance their social media presence and engage audiences through video content.
items.beforeBody:
Social media for musicians: grow your audience with videos by Ida Riegels from Nova AI

Musical Performances on Social Media Channels

The way musicians perform for audiences has changed, with social media taking up more and more attention. Although live performances are continuously thought to be the most special and valuable way to experience musical performances, the social media music scene is growing. Platforms like YouTube, TikTok, and others are already big gathering points for audiences with all kinds of different musical tastes and interests.

Digital improvements 

The sound and recording quality of digital devices has improved a lot over the years, and this enables everyone to create and listen to musical performances in relatively good quality, whether at home or out in public spaces.

An opportunity for musicians

Musical performances thereby have become much more accessible. For musicians, this is a great opportunity to share their content with audiences worldwide on a more regular basis and build a community.

This post will explore 5 creative ideas for a stronger social media presence for any musicians!

4 creative ideas for a stronger social media presence for musicians

1. Add lyrics to your videos

Have you ever played a song that originally has meaningful and deep lyrics? Many lullabies, pop, rock, and even metal songs have heartfelt lyrics and if you know the song well it’s almost like you can hear the words when the music plays.

But how about actually adding the words to your videos? To give your audience a better understanding of what the song is really about. It’s like adding subtitles or captions to videos to improve viewer focus and engagement.

If you think it might become a bit overwhelming with all the lyrics, try adding a few of the most meaningful sentences to your music videos.

2. Play in different locations and on different instruments or varieties of the same instrument

When creating videos, not only does the audible music part matter, but the visual settings are also part of the performance. And sometimes it can be exciting for your audience to see you play in new surroundings and on different types of the same instruments or different instruments entirely!

It’s great to have a stable base for playing and recording that you feel comfortable with and that your audience recognizes and feels familiar with. But, it can become a bit monotonous. If you can move around a bit, then why not try it out?

You might want to find a public spot somewhere and do some casual recordings. You could also try out a piano in a piano store. If you have a keyboard or a movable piano, you could take it for a trip, or ask someone you know with a piano if you can come and try it out. There are many creative ways to make your settings more special and unique.

3. Live stream your music on YouTube

In addition to traditional concerts, musicians can also make unique and customized concerts on YouTube through live-streaming. This allows musicians to be present with their audience and respond to their questions and requests. For example, a musician could perform a song that was requested by a fan or answer questions about their music. Live-streaming is a great way for musicians to connect with their fans and build stronger relationships.

If you play by ear, you can let your audience wish for specific songs in the chat, which can be great fun for both them and you. As a bonus, viewers can also donate money during the live-streaming.

4. Jump on the “Shorts” Wave

Today, short videos are very popular on platforms like YouTube, TikTok, and Facebook because people like to go on a short video spree. They are simply easier to watch!

Therefore, it can be beneficial to create both full-length videos and shorter video “tasters” to attract more people to your channels. Shorter videos are usually up to 60 seconds long.

The good thing is that short videos take less effort to create. You can practice only part of a song and then record it. Instead, you can take out the most emotional or outstanding piece of your recording and use it for a short video. This allows you to engage your viewers and create content regularly with ease. Therefore, to sum it up, being an ‘online’ musician can be lots of fun with the opportunity to be creative and reach and engage with audiences in all corners of the world.

items.title: 5 tips for left-handed drummers
items.editorNotes: Revised for clarity and readability while maintaining factual accuracy.
items.excerptBefore: Are you a lefty struggling to play the drums? Here are 5 Tips For Left-Handed Drummers that will improve your performance drastically!
items.excerptAfter: Discover essential tips for left-handed drummers to enhance their skills and adapt to a right-handed world.
items.seoTitleBefore: 5 tips for left-handed drummers
items.seoTitleAfter: 5 Tips for Left-Handed Drummers
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Are you a lefty struggling to play the drums? Here are 5 Tips For Left-Handed Drummers that will improve your performance drastically!
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Explore practical tips for left-handed drummers to improve their skills and navigate challenges in a predominantly right-handed world.
items.beforeBody:
Drumming As A Leftie

 

Are you a musician like me who happens to use their left hand dominantly while the rest of the world (90% of the population) uses their right hand? Left-handed people often get the short end of the stick when it comes to the tools available for them to use comfortably in the world. And certain instruments can provide a challenge for us as well.

The same applies to drumming. Since most drummers are right-handed, they typically arrange their drum sets in a way that may not be easy for left-handed drummers to adapt to if they want to play on the same setup.

The good thing is that drumming as a leftie is not impossible, and there are plenty of left-handed drummers out there who have found major success. Some notable names include Dominic Howard from Muse and Phil Collins.

If you’re down for a fun challenge, you may find that drumming as a leftie will give you even more exciting musical moments than you would have expected!

The 5 Best Tips:

Tip 1: Practice with a metronome

Whether you're a lefty or a righty, keeping rhythm is always an important priority.

A metronome helps you develop rhythm and tempo. This is essential for accuracy and precision in your music performance. If you are just starting out and want to improve your sense of rhythm while singing and playing, a tool like the Soundbrenner Core 2 can help. It can slow down the tempo to a manageable speed, and then gradually increase the tempo as you improve.

Designed for musicians by musicians, this vibrating metronome will revolutionize your practice sessions and keep you on beat all the time!

Additionally, the Soundbrenner Core 2 has an inbuilt vibrating metronome, contact tuner, dB meter and smartphone functions. This makes it any musician’s ultimate practice companion.

Learning the rhythm section by section at a slow speed helps you concentrate on the intricacies of the beat. The trick is to do things slowly and in baby steps. This way, you can keep the beat under your control and avoid making any mistakes.

Keep in mind that haste makes waste. Don't rush through learning the rhythm; instead, spend some time perfecting it at each tempo level.

Buy now

Tip 2: Know The Best Setup For Lefties

Most people set up drum kits for right-handed individuals, so left-handed drummers need to set up a mirrored kit. To avoid feeling uncomfortable and playing awkwardly on a right-handed kit, it's important tocommunicate their needs well in advance.

If you are practicing and recording on your own kit at home, there won’t be much of an issue. But when collaborating with right-handed musicians, you want to know what you will be dealing with in advance.

Tip 3: Connect With Other Lefty Drummers

An effective way to collaborate and stay in your comfortable left-handed world is to connect with other drummers that have the mirrored setup as well. You could swap tips and tricks for lefties that could help your music career in the long run.

Tip 4: Try Open-Handed Drumming

An example of a trick you can use as a lefty who drums is to play with the open-handed technique, which actually has several other benefits aside from being accessible to left-handed drummers. Playing this way involves not crossing your hands when you play the hi-hat, ride cymbal, or snare drum.

A lot of drummers shy away from the open-handed technique, but many right and left-handed folks who are seasoned agree that open-handed drumming has perks that make it well worth learning. Some say that playing open-handed just feels instinctively right to do.

Tip 5: Use The Kick More In Fills

A small trick to play more comfortably on a kit is to use the kick more when they perform fills instead of using their hands. This can make for smoother transitions for your body and for the sound of the music as well.

Bonus Tip: Study The Best Left-Handed Drummers

Connecting with other left-handed drummers may be a challenge, since lefties are hard to find in the first place. But you could always head to Google and YouTube for the inside scoop on some of the most renowned musicians out there who mastered the skill.

Celebrity musicians can often teach valuable skills just by performing, doing interviews, and providing behind-the-scenes material for their fans. Studying the great musicians that paved the way before us is always a great idea, no matter what instrument you play or what your dominant hand is.

 

Yona Marie Music authors this post, offering a wealth of useful tips for music makers worldwide who seek knowledge and experience in the industry.
